title: Ifloga lerouxiae
intro: Ifloga lerouxiae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Ifloga plant. It has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ifloga lerouxiae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been found to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ifloga lerouxiae include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Ifloga lerouxiae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the tribe Lactuceae within the Asteraceae family. This plant is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. Ifloga lerouxiae is a perennial herb that grows in a variety of habitats, including rocky outcrops and mountain slopes. It is a relatively rare and little-studied species.
